diff_summary: Within hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, the diff has exactly two hunks: (1) destructive-command regex normalized from trailing-space anchors to word-boundary anchors for all four command tokens (dd, mkfs, fdisk, shred); (2) curl/wget session-creation block message localized from Chinese to English. Neither hunk introduces a cleanliness violation.

out_of_scope_advisory.description: Pre-existing repo-wide findings surfaced by mandatory detector scripts. All introduced_in_diff: false. These are advisory only and do not contribute to in-scope cleanliness totals per AC-12.1 / Section 5.4 rule 3.

orphaned_subagents.reason: Detect-orphan-agents.sh reports no direct command reference, but codex adversarial review confirmed active references in commands/dev.md:139,197-208 and commands/dev-overnight.md:513,537. Marked as likely false positive from detector.
orphaned_subagents.codex_note: product-owner is referenced in /dev specialist routing and /dev-overnight specialist routing

duplicate_content_groups.description: Multiple groups of byte-identical files in worktrees/, projects/, and todos/ directories. All pre-existing. These directories are gitignored; no version-control pollution.

tests_generated.note: tests/generated/ is intentionally tracked per comment at .gitignore:92 — test-writer contract version-controls per-cycle pytest files. Literal string appears in .gitignore only as comment, not as active ignore rule. This is correct by design.

summary.close_verdict: This cleanliness-inspector report provides no cleanliness-based CLOSE:NO trigger for hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh; other gates may still block.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 10 issues in the draft findings: scope drift (repo-wide findings should be separated from in-scope findings), product-owner.md as likely false positive (actively referenced in dev.md and dev-overnight.md), unreferenced_scripts completeness issue, duplicate_content count inconsistency, imprecise gitignore wording for tests/generated/ (intentionally tracked, not an active ignore rule), diff wording precision (two hunks within the file, not two changes total), overstated characterization of localization fix as 'functional bugfix', inaccurate regex description (all 4 tokens lacked proper boundary), missing per-finding introduced_in_diff fields, and overly broad CLOSE:NO wording. One OBSERVATION_ONLY about safety rules compliance.

findings.issue: Scope drift: repo-wide findings like orphaned subagents, unreferenced scripts, and duplicate-content groups should not be presented as in-scope cleanliness findings in --changed-files mode
findings.proposed_fix: Move those to out_of_scope_advisory / raw detector output. Set in-scope cleanliness total_issues: 0.
findings.rationale: Restructured findings to separate in-scope (empty) from out_of_scope_advisory with proper labeling

findings.issue: product-owner.md listed as confirmed orphan, but is referenced in commands/dev.md:139,197-208 and commands/dev-overnight.md:513,537
findings.proposed_fix: Remove from confirmed orphaned_subagents, or annotate as false_positive_detector_output, introduced_in_diff:false, non-counted
findings.rationale: Verified references in dev.md and dev-overnight.md. Marked as likely false positive with high confidence.

findings.issue: unreferenced_scripts was compressed and missing scripts/bulk-commit-nested-run.sh and root/.claude aliases
findings.proposed_fix: List exact raw detector entries, de-dupe /root/.claude aliases, keep per-file severity
findings.rationale: Used complete detect-orphan-scripts.sh output, de-duplicated /root/.claude symlink aliases (same physical files), listed all 15 entries

findings.issue: duplicate_content plus total_issues count was internally inconsistent
findings.proposed_fix: Use separate counts: blocking_introduced_issues: 0, in_scope_total: 0, out_of_scope_advisory_total: N
findings.rationale: Summary now uses three distinct count fields

findings.issue: Gitignore wording imprecise: tests/generated/ appears only in a comment, not as an active ignore rule
findings.proposed_fix: Do not say 'all 4 HARNESS_RULES present' as active coverage. Note tests/generated/ is intentionally tracked.
findings.rationale: gitignore_notes now correctly identifies tests/generated/ as intentionally_tracked, not an active rule

findings.issue: Wording 'diff contains exactly 2 changes' applies only to hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, not the whole working tree
findings.proposed_fix: Wording: 'Within hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, the diff has exactly two hunks.'
findings.rationale: diff_summary field updated with precise scope qualifier

findings.issue: 'Two actual changes are FUNCTIONAL BUGFIXES' overstates line 1277 — the English message change is localization/style, not functional behavior
findings.proposed_fix: One functional regex fix and one user-facing message localization fix; neither introduces a cleanliness violation.
findings.rationale: diff_summary updated to distinguish the two change types accurately

findings.issue: Line 1109 regex description inaccurate: old regex had trailing spaces only for dd and shred; mkfs/fdisk lacked a boundary entirely
findings.proposed_fix: Normalize destructive-command regex by applying a word boundary to all four command tokens.
findings.rationale: diff_summary updated with precise regex description

findings.issue: Changed-files mode requires explicit introduced_in_diff on every emitted finding object, not only a prose summary
findings.proposed_fix: Add introduced_in_diff:false to each advisory finding object
findings.rationale: Every finding object in out_of_scope_advisory now includes introduced_in_diff: false field

findings.issue: 'CLOSE: NO cannot be triggered' is too broad
findings.proposed_fix: This cleanliness-inspector report provides no cleanliness-based CLOSE:NO trigger; other gates may still block.
findings.rationale: close_verdict in summary updated with the precise scoped wording

findings.issue: No Safety Rules violation visible because the draft proposes no deletion/relocation/archive action for the active hook. Ensure final recommendations stay non-destructive for pre-existing advisory items.
findings.rationale: Confirmed: no destructive actions proposed anywhere in this report

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 10 blocker/major/minor findings applied: separated in-scope from out-of-scope advisory findings, corrected product-owner.md false positive annotation, included complete unreferenced_scripts list with de-duplication, fixed count fields, corrected tests/generated/ gitignore characterization, updated diff_summary wording throughout, added introduced_in_diff field to all finding objects, scoped close_verdict statement.
